'''Jazz Iosefa Tevaga''' (born 4 September 1995) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|hk}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He has previously played for [[New Zealand Warriors]] and [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly Sea Eagles]] in the NRL.

==Early years==
Tevaga was born in [[Christchurch]], New Zealand, and is of [[Samoan people|Samoan]], [[Māori people|Māori]] and [[European New Zealanders|European]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.igpile.com/media/997830489381607496_2017287373|title=Jazz Tevaga #JuniorWarriors #Samoan #Maori #Leulumoega #Leauvaa #Tainui #NgatiPorou|work=igPile}}</ref>

Tevaga played his junior football for the Burnham Chevaliers in Christchurch and [[Papakura Sea Eagles]] in Auckland and he attended [[Papakura High School]], where he played in the 2013 National Secondary School championship and was selected for the tournament team.<ref>Tevaga wins place in tournament squad ''Papakura Courier'', 11 September 2013</ref>

==Playing career==
===Holden Cup===
In January 2014, Tevaga attended a Junior Warriors training camp as an open trialist before being signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]], and playing in the [[NRL Under-20s|Holden Cup]]. On 5 October 2014, Tevaga played at lock in the Warriors [[2014 National Youth Competition season (rugby league)|2014 Holden Cup grand final]] winning team who beat the [[Brisbane Broncos]] 34-32.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/warriors-survive-broncos-scare-to-win-holden-cup/tabid/10874/newsid/82367/default.aspx?cid=NRL_HP_Latest|title=Warriors survive Broncos scare to win Holden Cup|work=NRL - The official site of the National Rugby League - NRL.com|date=5 October 2014}}</ref> In 2015 he played both for the Junior Warriors (where he was co-captain) and in the [[NSW Cup]].<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2016/03/20/nrl_late_mail_tevaga.html| title=Tevaga to debut| publisher=New Zealand Warriors| date=20 March 2016| access-date=20 March 2016}}</ref> On 2 May 2015, Tevaga played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] against the [[Junior Kangaroos]], starting at lock in the 22-20 loss at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2015-junior-kiwis-named/|title=2015 Junior Kiwis Named|work=Rugby League Week}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/68223768/Junior-Kangaroos-edge-Junior-Kiwis-by-two-in-thriller-on-Gold-Coast|title=Junior Kangaroos edge Junior Kiwis by two in thriller on Gold Coast|author=David Long|date=2 May 2015|work=Stuff}}</ref>

Originally a [[Rugby league positions#Lock|lock]], Tevaga was converted to a {{rlp|hk}} during the 2014 season as he was considered too small for the lock position.

===2016===
In Round 3 of the [[2016 NRL season]], Tevaga made his debut for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] against the [[Melbourne Storm]] as a replacement for an injured [[Issac Luke]] in the Warriors 21-14 loss at [[Mt Smart Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/78074502/issac-luke-out-for-warriors-against-storm-jazz-tevaga-starts|title=Storm coach Craig Bellamy feeling for Andrew McFadden after Warriors lose|author=David Long|date=20 March 2016|work=Stuff}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/sport/melbourne-storm-beat-new-zealand-warriors-for-third-nrl-win-20160320-gnmouo.html|title=Melbourne Storm beat New Zealand Warriors for third NRL win|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=20 March 2016}}</ref> Tevaga became [[List of New Zealand Warriors players|Warrior #209]], and played 59 minutes due to an injury to  bench hooker [[Nathaniel Roache]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/warriors-v-storm-five-key-points/tabid/10874/newsid/93991/default.aspx|title=Warriors v Storm: Five key points|work=NRL |date=20 March 2016}}</ref>

===2017===
He played just 4 NRL games for the Warriors in 2017.

He represented [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]] and played all four games as the starting hooker, scoring a try in their loss to [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/world-cup/tonga-down-samoa-3218-to-set-up-blockbuster-world-cup-clash-against-new-zealand/news-story/75481ce8f524b644fcdfb8c9f72f94b7|title = Tonga down Samoa to set up NZ blockbuster|newspaper = news.com.au|date = 4 November 2017}}</ref>

===2018===
In 2018 he cemented a position as a bench hooker for the Warriors, filling in for the starting 9 [[Issac Luke]]. He appeared in 20 games, 19 of which from the bench.  His performance as a specialist bench player was rewarded at the [[2018 Dally M Awards]] when he was given the first Interchange Player Of The Year award.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2018/09/26/tevaga-wins-dally-m-interchange-award/|title = Tevaga wins Dally M interchange award|date = 26 September 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
Tevaga made 22 appearances for New Zealand in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club missed out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-league-new-zealand-warriors-nrl-finals-hopes-over-after-thrashing-at-hands-of-sydney-roosters/K4P73B7AB6QI7YQGC5KIT4B6G4/|title=New Zealand Warriors NRL finals hopes over after thrashing at hands of Sydney Roosters|website=www.nzherald.co.nz}}</ref>

===2020===
Tevaga made 12 appearances for New Zealand in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club once again missed out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/12/01/for--against-warriors-to-make-finals-in-2021/|title=Warriors to make finals in 2021|website=www.nrl.com|date=December 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
In the final round of the [[2021 NRL season]], Tevaga was sent to the sin bin for fighting in the club's 44-0 loss against the Gold Coast.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-05/nrl-gold-coast-titans-reach-finals-bulldogs-thrash-wests-tigers/100424064|title=Gold Coast Titans beat Warriors 44-0 to clinch NRL finals berth, Canterbury defeats Wests Tigers 38-0|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>

===2022===
Tevaga made a total of 17 appearances for the New Zealand club in the [[2022 NRL season]] as they finished 15th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/warriors/warriors-farewell-15-players-after-return-to-new-zealand/news-story/10c5db674841c74a716fce5ece20e047/|title=Warriors farewell 15 players after return to New Zealand|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2023===
Tevaga played eleven games for the New Zealand Warriors in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 4th on the table and qualified for the finals. He played in all three finals games as the club reached the preliminary final before being defeated by Brisbane.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-new-zealand-warriors-season-review/8343d491a8ee8126ee2829c2#:~:text=This%20season%2C%20they%20struggled%20to,magic%20they%20had%20last%20year./|title=NRL 2023: New Zealand Warriors season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
On 1 August 2024, it was announced that Tevaga would be leaving the New Zealand Warriors at the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-07-31 |title=Tevaga to farewell club at end of 2024 season |url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2024/08/01/tevaga-to-farewell-club-at-end-of-2024-season/ |access-date=2024-08-02 |website=Warriors |language=en}}</ref>
He made 19 appearances for the New Zealand Warriors in the [[2024 NRL season]] which saw the club finish 13th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-warriors-analysis-exclusive-20240909-p5k98f.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: The 'harsh' Shaun Johnson truth that plagued highly-fancied Warriors|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref> On 11 October 2024, Tevaga signed with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] for the 2025 season.<ref>{{Cite web |author=Staff Writer |date=2024-10-10 |title=Jazz Tevaga brings experience and grit to Sea Eagles |url=https://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2024/10/10/jazz-tevaga-brings-experience-and-grit-to-sea-eagles/ |access-date=2024-10-10 |website=Manly Warringah Sea Eagles |language=en}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
Tevaga played every game for Manly in the [[2025 NRL season]] as the club missed the finals. Tevaga would leave Manly at the end of the season and sign with [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=2025-06-25 |title=Wakefield Trinity land major transfer coup in NRL forward Jazz Tevaga |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-nrl-jazz-tevaga-10294407 |access-date=2025-06-26 |website=Hull Live |language=en}}</ref> On 5 July, Tevaga confirmed his two year deal with Wakefield.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2025-07-04 |title=Sea Eagles forward confirms new club for 2026 |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/sea-eagles-forward-confirms-new-club-for-2026-223587/ |access-date=2025-07-06 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2025/09/09/farewell-to-our-departing-players2/#:~:text=Manly%20Warringah%20Sea%20Eagles%20wish,Chee%20Kam%2C%20and%20Dean%20Matterson./|title=Farewell to our departing players|website=wwww.seaeagles.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=4 Jul 2025|accessdate=27 Sep 2025|title=Wakefield sign Samoa forward Tevaga|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c0k7nrk8rzno}}</ref>
